rationale: Hard pass for TheSphinx. The bright pastel/green-blue jigsaw palette and tactile drag-and-drop satisfaction hook directly contradict the game's dark mystical-minimalism aesthetic, and the mechanic (spatial assembly) has zero overlap with voice-driven yes/no deduction about a hidden celebrity. Audience also skews casual relax-style puzzlers (often older/female-skewed jigsaw players) rather than the educated 25-45 intellectual-challenge crowd — don't adapt this archetype, the only salvageable idea would be replacing the jigsaw with a "piece-by-piece celebrity reveal" concept, which is effectively a different creative.
